nodes.context: Corsican organized crime syndicate active in the French Connection heroin trade from 1930s to 1970s.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate, more powerful than Unione Corse.
Sicilian organized crime syndicate involved in the French Connection.
Italian organized crime syndicate from Campania, mentioned as former source of heroin.

edges.evidence_quote: those clans remained overshadowed by the much more powerful Italian-American Mafia
edges.context: Unione Corse clans were less powerful than the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: they rather constituted the local segment of a vast international network and were commercial partners, or even subcontractors, of the much more powerful Italian-American Cosa Nostra
edges.context: Unione Corse acted as commercial partners or subcontractors for the American Mafia in the French Connection.

edges.evidence_quote: the latter did not control the whole traffic and participated in it in response to a call for tender from the Sicilians in New York
edges.context: Unione Corse participated in the French Connection at the request of Sicilian Mafia in New York.

edges.evidence_quote: previous source, legal Italian pharmaceutical production hijacked by the Camorra, was shut down in 1951
edges.context: Camorra was a previous source of heroin for the network before being shut down.

edges.evidence_quote: structured by mobster Lucky Luciano and the Italian-American mafia after their previous source... was shut down
edges.context: American Mafia and Sicilian Mafia collaborated in structuring the French Connection.
